---
title: "Urban Air Quality Research"
output: pdf_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
toc: "true"
toc_depth: 3
toc_float: "true"
number_sections: "true"
fig_caption: "true"
theme: "readable"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
# 1. Data Loading and Preparation

## 1.1 EPA Site Data
## 1.2 CLEANinCLE Sensor Data

# 2. EPA Site-to-Site PM2.5 Matrix

# 3. CinC-to-CinC Pairwise Matrix

# 4. EPA vs CinC Comparisons

## 4.1 Daily Averages by Site
## 4.2 Filter Days Only

# 5. Sensor Agreement vs Distance

# 6. Maps and Site Locations
```

## Objectives
	•	Prepare and reshape the EPA dataset into a clean, wide format
	•	Generate site-by-site PM2.5 comparison plots with regression lines and R² values
	•	Build a matrix plot of all pairwise site comparisons
	•	Analyze relationships between R² and geographic distance between sites


## Libraries

```{r libs}

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(geosphere)
library(GGally)
library(gridExtra)
library(broom)
library(sf)
library(ggrepel)

```


# EPA data - load & organize
```{r epa-load}
epa_sites <- c("0034","0038","0045","0060","0065","0073")

read_epa_site <- function(site){
  f <- sprintf("epa_pm25_site_%s.csv", site)
  stopifnot(file.exists(f))
  read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(site_number = site)
}

epa_all <- map_dfr(epa_sites, read_epa_site)

# Quick sanity peek
epa_all %>% count(site_number, sample_duration)
```

## 6x6 MATRIX PLOT FOR CONTINOUS DATA
```{r epa-6x6-hourly-grid, fig.width=20, fig.height=20, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

# Step 1: Create a list of site data frames
site_dfs <- list(
  "0034" = df_0034,
  "0038" = df_0038,
  "0045" = df_0045,
  "0060" = df_0060,
  "0065" = df_0065,
  "0073" = df_0073
)

# Step 2: Generate daily averages from hourly data
epa_hourly_daily_avg <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(site_dfs), function(site) {
    df <- site_dfs[[site]]
    df %>%
      filter(sample_duration == "1 HOUR") %>%
      group_by(date_local) %>%
      summarise(pm25 = mean(sample_measurement, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      mutate(site_number = site, date = as.Date(date_local)) %>%
      select(date, site_number, pm25)
  })
)

# Step 3: Pivot to wide format
epa_hourly_wide <- epa_hourly_daily_avg %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = site_number, values_from = pm25, values_fn = mean) %>%
  filter(rowSums(is.na(across(-date))) < 3)

# Step 4: Set up for matrix plot
site_names <- sort(setdiff(names(epa_hourly_wide), "date"))
n_sites <- length(site_names)
axis_min <- 0
axis_max <- 28

plot_matrix <- vector("list", n_sites * n_sites)

for (i in seq_len(n_sites)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n_sites)) {
    x_site <- site_names[j]
    y_site <- site_names[i]
    index <- (i - 1) * n_sites + j

    df_pair <- epa_hourly_wide %>%
      select(x = all_of(x_site), y = all_of(y_site)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y))

    if (nrow(df_pair) > 2) {
      model <- lm(y ~ x, data = df_pair)
      r2 <- round(summary(model)$r.squared, 2)
      slope <- round(coef(model)[2], 2)
      intercept <- round(coef(model)[1], 2)
      label_text <- paste0("y = ", slope, "x + ", intercept, "\nR² = ", r2)
    } else {
      label_text <- "Insufficient data"
    }

    x_pos <- axis_min + 1
    y_pos <- axis_max - 1

    p <- ggplot(df_pair, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
      geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
      coord_fixed(xlim = c(axis_min, axis_max), ylim = c(axis_min, axis_max)) +
      labs(x = paste("Site", x_site), y = paste("Site", y_site)) +
      annotate("text", x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = label_text, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
      )

    plot_matrix[[index]] <- p
  }
}

# Step 5: Display matrix plot
grid_plot <- grid.arrange(grobs = plot_matrix, ncol = n_sites, nrow = n_sites)

print(grid_plot)
```

# Daily FRM (24‑hr) & FEM (hourly→daily)
```{r epa-averages}
epa_24hr_daily <- epa_all %>%
  filter(sample_duration == "24 HOUR") %>%
  group_by(site_number, date_local) %>%
  summarise(pm25 = mean(sample_measurement, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  transmute(Date = as.Date(date_local), Site = site_number, PM25 = pm25)

epa_hourly_daily <- epa_all %>%
  filter(sample_duration == "1 HOUR") %>%
  group_by(site_number, date_local) %>%
  summarise(pm25 = mean(sample_measurement, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  transmute(Date = as.Date(date_local), Site = site_number, PM25 = pm25)

# Site coordinates (averaged across file rows)
epa_coords <- epa_all %>%
  select(site_number, latitude, longitude) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(site_number) %>%
  summarise(
    Latitude = mean(latitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    Longitude = mean(longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(Site = site_number)

# Join FRM + FEM by Site & Date
epa_frm <- epa_24hr_daily   %>% rename(PM25_FRM = PM25)   # Date, Site, PM25_FRM
epa_fem <- epa_hourly_daily %>% rename(PM25_FEM = PM25)   # Date, Site, PM25_FEM

epa_join <- inner_join(epa_fem, epa_frm, by = c("Date","Site")) %>%
  filter(is.finite(PM25_FEM), is.finite(PM25_FRM))

# Robust shared limits for figures
lim_hi <- quantile(c(epa_join$PM25_FEM, epa_join$PM25_FRM), probs = 0.995, na.rm = TRUE)
lim_hi <- max(5, ceiling(lim_hi/5)*5)
lims   <- c(0, lim_hi)
lims
```

# 6x6 MATRIX PLOT OF FILTER DATA COMPARISONS.
```{r epa-6x6-filter-grid, fig.width=22, fig.height=22, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

# List of site numbers and their corresponding dataframes
site_dfs <- list(
  "0034" = df_0034,
  "0038" = df_0038,
  "0045" = df_0045,
  "0060" = df_0060,
  "0065" = df_0065,
  "0073" = df_0073
)

# Create a combined daily average dataframe
epa_24hr_clean <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(site_dfs), function(site) {
    df <- site_dfs[[site]]
    df %>%
      filter(sample_duration == "24 HOUR") %>%
      group_by(date_local) %>%
      summarise(pm25 = mean(sample_measurement, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      mutate(site_number = site, date = as.Date(date_local)) %>%
      select(date, site_number, pm25)
  })
)

# Prepare and reshape the data into wide format
epa_wide <- epa_24hr_clean %>%
  select(date, site_number, pm25) %>%
  mutate(site_number = as.character(site_number)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = site_number, values_from = pm25, values_fn = mean) %>%
  filter(rowSums(is.na(across(-date))) < 3)

# Set up plotting variables
site_names <- sort(setdiff(names(epa_wide), "date"))
n_sites <- length(site_names)
axis_min <- 0
axis_max <- 28

# Create matrix of plots
plot_matrix <- vector("list", n_sites * n_sites)

for (i in seq_len(n_sites)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n_sites)) {
    x_site <- site_names[j]
    y_site <- site_names[i]
    index <- (i - 1) * n_sites + j

    df_pair <- epa_wide %>%
      select(x = all_of(x_site), y = all_of(y_site)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y))

    if (nrow(df_pair) > 2) {
      model <- lm(y ~ x, data = df_pair)
      r2 <- round(summary(model)$r.squared, 2)
      slope <- round(coef(model)[2], 2)
      intercept <- round(coef(model)[1], 2)
      label_text <- paste0("y = ", slope, "x + ", intercept, "\nR² = ", r2)
    } else {
      label_text <- "Insufficient data"
    }

    # Place label in top-left corner of the plot
    x_pos <- axis_min + 1
    y_pos <- axis_max - 1

    p <- ggplot(df_pair, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
      geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
      coord_fixed(xlim = c(axis_min, axis_max), ylim = c(axis_min, axis_max)) +
      labs(x = paste("Site", x_site), y = paste("Site", y_site)) +
      annotate("text", x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = label_text, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
      )

    plot_matrix[[index]] <- p
  }
}

# Display the 6x6 plot matrix
grid_plot <- grid.arrange(grobs = plot_matrix, ncol = n_sites, nrow = n_sites)

print(grid_plot)
```



# EPA Continuous (FEM daily avg) vs EPA FRM (24‑hr) 
```{r epa-6panel, fig.width=13, fig.height=10}
library(gridExtra)

panel_one_site <- function(site_id, lims, breaks_n = 6) {
  df <- epa_join %>% filter(Site == site_id)

  lab <- if (nrow(df) > 2) {
    fit <- lm(PM25_FEM ~ PM25_FRM, data = df)
    sprintf("y = %.2fx %+ .2f\nR² = %.2f",
            coef(fit)[2], coef(fit)[1], summary(fit)$r.squared)
  } else {
    "n < 3"
  }

  ggplot(df, aes(PM25_FRM, PM25_FEM)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.80, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.0, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    annotate("text",
             x = lims[1] + diff(lims)*0.03,
             y = lims[2] - diff(lims)*0.06,
             label = lab, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
    coord_equal(xlim = lims, ylim = lims, expand = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = site_id,
      x = expression("EPA FRM PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)"),
      y = expression("EPA Continuous PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)")
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(breaks_n)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(breaks_n)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

site_list   <- c("0034","0038","0045","0060","0065","0073")
panel_plots <- lapply(site_list, panel_one_site, lims = lims, breaks_n = 6)

title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
  "EPA Continuous (FEM daily avg) vs EPA FRM (24hr) — by site",
  gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold")
)

p_epa_fem_vs_frm <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(grobs = panel_plots, ncol = 3, top = title_grob)

dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("figs/fig_epa_fem_vs_frm_by_site_numbered_axes.png",
       p_epa_fem_vs_frm, width = 13, height = 10, dpi = 320)

grid::grid.newpage(); grid::grid.draw(p_epa_fem_vs_frm)
```

# Data exported used for Fig A (Excel)
```{r export-fig-table}
site_limits <- epa_join %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(
    panel_min = lims[1],
    panel_max = lims[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

figA_table <- epa_join %>%
  left_join(site_limits, by = "Site") %>%
  arrange(Site, Date)

readr::write_csv(figA_table, "figs/figA_epa_fem_vs_frm_table.csv")

# Preview
figA_table %>% group_by(Site) %>% slice_head(n = 5)

```

# Scatterplot of EPA FRM vs EPA Continuous PM2.5 with regression
```{r}
readr::write_csv(figA_table, "figs/figA_epa_fem_vs_frm_table.csv")

figA_table <- epa_join %>%
  left_join(site_limits, by = "Site") %>%
  arrange(Site, Date)

lab <- if (nrow(df) > 2) {
    fit <- lm(PM25_FEM ~ PM25_FRM, data = df)
    sprintf("y = %.2fx %+ .2f\nR² = %.2f",
            coef(fit)[2], coef(fit)[1], summary(fit)$r.squared)
  } else {
    "n < 3"
  }

ggplot(df, aes(PM25_FRM, PM25_FEM)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.80, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.0, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    annotate("text",
             x = lims[1] + diff(lims)*0.03,
             y = lims[2] - diff(lims)*0.06,
             label = lab, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
    coord_equal(xlim = lims, ylim = lims, expand = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = site_id,
      x = expression("EPA FRM PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)"),
      y = expression("EPA Continuous PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)")
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(breaks_n)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(breaks_n)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
```

# EPA FEM vs FRM 6-panel comparison plot
```{r epa-6panel, fig.width=13, fig.height=10}

stopifnot(all(c("Site","Date","PM25_FRM","PM25_FEM","panel_min","panel_max") %in% names(figA_table)))

# 1) pull the common limits from the table
lims <- c(unique(figA_table$panel_min)[1], unique(figA_table$panel_max)[1])

# 2) per‑site regression stats (for panel labels)
site_labs_figA <- figA_table %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Site) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n         = dplyr::n(),
    slope     = if (n() > 2) coef(lm(PM25_FEM ~ PM25_FRM))[2] else NA_real_,
    intercept = if (n() > 2) coef(lm(PM25_FEM ~ PM25_FRM))[1] else NA_real_,
    r2        = if (n() > 2) summary(lm(PM25_FEM ~ PM25_FRM))$r.squared else NA_real_,
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    label = ifelse(is.finite(r2),
                   sprintf("y = %.2fx %+ .2f\nR² = %.2f", slope, intercept, r2),
                   "n < 3"),
    x = lims[1] + diff(lims)*0.03,
    y = lims[2] - diff(lims)*0.06
  )

# 3) plot from figA_table
p_figA <- ggplot(figA_table, aes(PM25_FRM, PM25_FEM)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.80, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.0, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  coord_equal(xlim = lims, ylim = lims, expand = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Site, ncol = 3) +
  geom_text(
    data = site_labs_figA,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "EPA Continuous (FEM daily avg) vs EPA FRM (24‑hr)-by site",
    x = expression("EPA FRM PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)"),
    y = expression("EPA Continuous PM"[2.5]*" (µg/m³)")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.02),
    strip.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title  = element_text(size = 13),  # (your reduced label size)
    axis.text   = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# 4) save + print
dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("figs/figA_epa_fem_vs_frm_from_table.png", p_figA, width = 13, height = 10, dpi = 320)
p_figA
```

# EPA site‑to‑site agreement vs distance
```{r epa-r2-vs-distance, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}

compute_r2_vs_distance <- function(wide_df, coords_df){
  sites <- sort(setdiff(names(wide_df), "Date"))
  out <- list()

  for (i in seq_along(sites)) for (j in seq_along(sites)) {
    s1 <- sites[i]; s2 <- sites[j]
    dfp <- wide_df %>% select(x = all_of(s1), y = all_of(s2)) %>% drop_na()
    r2 <- if (nrow(dfp) > 2) summary(lm(y ~ x, data = dfp))$r.squared else NA_real_

    c1 <- coords_df %>% filter(Site == s1)
    c2 <- coords_df %>% filter(Site == s2)
    dist_km <- distHaversine(c(c1$Longitude, c1$Latitude), c(c2$Longitude, c2$Latitude)) / 1000

    out[[length(out)+1]] <- tibble(Site1 = s1, Site2 = s2, Distance_km = dist_km, R2 = r2)
  }

  bind_rows(out) %>%
    filter(Site1 != Site2) %>%
    rowwise() %>% mutate(pair_id = paste(sort(c(Site1, Site2)), collapse = "-")) %>%
    ungroup() %>% distinct(pair_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% select(-pair_id)
}

# Build wide tables for this helper
epa_24hr_wide <- epa_24hr_daily %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Site, values_from = PM25)
epa_hourly_wide <- epa_hourly_daily %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Site, values_from = PM25)

epa_r2dist_24hr   <- compute_r2_vs_distance(epa_24hr_wide,  epa_coords)
epa_r2dist_hourly <- compute_r2_vs_distance(epa_hourly_wide, epa_coords)

p1 <- ggplot(epa_r2dist_24hr, aes(Distance_km, R2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "EPA 24hr PM2.5: Inter‑site Agreement vs Distance",
       x = "Distance (km)", y = expression(R^2)) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("figs/fig_epa_r2_vs_distance_24hr.png", p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
p1
```

# Map: EPA sites+CLEANinCLE devices+Cleveland boundary
```{r map-rds, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Load saved files you already created
target_counties   <- readRDS("target_counties.rds")
lake_erie         <- readRDS("lake_erie.rds")
epa_sites_map     <- readRDS("epa_sites.rds")         
cleanincle_sites  <- readRDS("cleanincle_sites.rds")
cleveland_land    <- readRDS("cleveland_land.rds")

# Color Cuyahoga
target_counties$fill_color <- ifelse(target_counties$NAME == "Cuyahoga", "#dfffe1", "lightgrey")

# Legend setup
legend_order <- c("EPA AQS Sites", "CLEANinCLE Sites", "Cleveland Boundary")
epa_sites_map$type        <- factor("EPA AQS Sites", levels = legend_order)
cleanincle_sites$type     <- factor("CLEANinCLE Sites", levels = legend_order)
cleveland_land$type       <- factor("Cleveland Boundary", levels = legend_order)

# Choose the right label column for site numbers (adjust if your column differs)
label_col <- dplyr::coalesce(epa_sites_map$site_number,
                             epa_sites_map$Site,
                             epa_sites_map$aqs_site_id)
epa_sites_map$label_txt <- as.character(label_col)

# Labels for big features
map_labels <- tibble::tibble(
  label = c("Lake Erie", "Cuyahoga County"),
  longitude = c(-81.85, -81.743),
  latitude  = c(41.62,  41.37)
)

p_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = target_counties, aes(fill = fill_color), color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = lake_erie, fill = "#d9edff", color = NA, show.legend = FALSE) +

  geom_point(data = epa_sites_map, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, color = type),
             shape = 16, size = 2.6, show.legend = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = cleanincle_sites, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, color = type),
             shape = 4, size = 2.6, stroke = 1, show.legend = TRUE) +

  geom_sf(data = cleveland_land, aes(color = type),
          linetype = 1, linewidth = 1, fill = NA, show.legend = TRUE) +

  geom_text(data = map_labels, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, label = label),
            color = "black", size = 5, fontface = "italic") +

  # Repelled labels for *EPA site numbers*
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = epa_sites_map, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, label = label_txt),
    seed = 123, size = 3.6, fontface = "bold",
    color = "black", box.padding = 0.25, point.padding = 0.25, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                     values = c("EPA AQS Sites" = "red",
                                "CLEANinCLE Sites" = "darkblue",
                                "Cleveland Boundary" = "purple")) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
    shape = c(16, 4, NA),
    linetype = c(0, 0, 1),
    size = c(2.6, 2.6, 1)
  ))) +

  scale_fill_identity() +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-82.1, -81.3), ylim = c(41.27, 41.65)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")

dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("figs/map_cleveland_numbered.png", p_map, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 320)
p_map
```

# Map of EPA sites and CLEANinCLE devices in Cuyahoga County
```{r map-epa-cinc, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(tigris)   
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE, tigris_class = "sf")

# --- 1) Convert your coordinates to sf points (WGS84) ---
epa_sf <- epa_coords %>%
  mutate(SiteLab = Site) %>%              # e.g., "0034"
  st_as_sf(coords = c("Longitude","Latitude"), crs = 4326)

cinc_sf <- cinc_locations %>%
  mutate(DeviceLab = as.character(device_number)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude","latitude"), crs = 4326)

# --- 2) Get background shapes: Cuyahoga County + Cleveland city ---
ohio_counties <- counties(state = "OH", year = 2023) %>% st_transform(4326)
cuyahoga      <- ohio_counties %>% filter(NAME == "Cuyahoga")

ohio_places   <- places(state = "OH", year = 2023) %>% st_transform(4326)
cleveland     <- ohio_places %>% filter(NAME == "Cleveland")

# --- 3) Build the map ---
p_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = cuyahoga, fill = "honeydew", color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_sf(data = cleveland, fill = NA, color = "purple", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # CLEANinCLE devices (blue X)
  geom_sf(data = cinc_sf, color = "navy", shape = 4, stroke = 1.1, size = 3) +
  # EPA sites (red dots)
  geom_sf(data = epa_sf, color = "red3", fill = "red3", shape = 21, size = 3) +
  # EPA labels (site numbers)
  geom_label_repel(
    data = epa_sf, aes(label = SiteLab, geometry = geometry),
    stat = "sf_coordinates", seed = 123,
    size = 3.4, label.size = 0.2, label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),
    min.segment.length = 0, box.padding = 0.25
  ) +
  # optional: show fewer CinC labels by sampling (uncomment to label all if wanted)
  # geom_text_repel(
  #   data = cinc_sf, aes(label = DeviceLab, geometry = geometry),
  #   stat = "sf_coordinates", size = 2.6, color = "navy", seed = 123, box.padding = 0.15
  # ) +
  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "EPA AQS Sites and CLEANinCLE Devices in Cuyahoga County",
       subtitle = "Red = EPA sites (with site numbers), Blue X = CLEANinCLE devices\nPurple outline = City of Cleveland",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )

# --- 4) Save & print ---
dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave("figs/map_epa_cinc_cuyahoga.png", p_map, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 320)
p_map
```

# CLEANinCLE Data loading and preprocessing
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(geosphere)
library(broom)


cinc_path <- "~/Documents/R_Projects/Air_pollution_Projects/CLEANinCLE_Raw_Air_Quality_Data_2024-11-2025-05.csv"
stopifnot(file.exists(cinc_path))

cinc_raw <- readr::read_csv(cinc_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Normalize names once
names(cinc_raw) <- tolower(names(cinc_raw))

# Keep PM2.5 rows (common names seen so far)
pm25_rows <- cinc_raw %>%
  filter(measure_name %in% c("pm25","pm2.5","mc_pm2_5"))

# Daily averages per device
cinc_daily <- pm25_rows %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(time, quiet = TRUE),
         date     = as.Date(datetime)) %>%
  transmute(date,
            device_number = as.character(device_number),
            pm25 = value,
            latitude, longitude,
            device_eui, location_name) %>%
  group_by(device_number, date) %>%
  summarise(pm25 = mean(pm25, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

# One row per device (for mapping)
cinc_locations <- pm25_rows %>%
  transmute(device_number = as.character(device_number),
            device_eui, location_name,
            latitude, longitude) %>%
  distinct()
```

# Closest EPA sites vs. CinC
```{r build-nearest-epa, message=FALSE}
# Requires epa_coords from your earlier EPA section
#   epa_coords: Site, Latitude, Longitude
stopifnot(all(c("Site","Latitude","Longitude") %in% names(epa_coords)))

# Map each CinC device to its nearest EPA site
device_to_epa <- cinc_locations %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    nearest_epa = epa_coords$Site[ which.min(
      distHaversine(c(longitude, latitude),
                    cbind(epa_coords$Longitude, epa_coords$Latitude))
    ) ],
    dist_km = min(
      distHaversine(c(longitude, latitude),
                    cbind(epa_coords$Longitude, epa_coords$Latitude))
    )/1000
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(epa_site = nearest_epa)

# Join CinC daily with nearest EPA daily (both FRM & FEM→daily)
epa_24hr_daily_std   <- epa_24hr_daily   %>% rename(pm25_epa_frm = PM25)   # Date, Site, pm25_epa_frm
epa_hourly_daily_std <- epa_hourly_daily %>% rename(pm25_epa_fem = PM25)   # Date, Site, pm25_epa_fem

cinc_daily_map <- cinc_daily %>%
  left_join(device_to_epa %>% select(device_number, epa_site, dist_km), by = "device_number")

# A) CinC vs EPA FRM (24‑hr)
cinc_vs_epa_frm <- cinc_daily_map %>%
  inner_join(epa_24hr_daily_std, by = c("epa_site" = "Site", "date" = "Date")) %>%
  rename(pm25_cinc = pm25)

# B) CinC vs EPA FEM (hourly→daily)
cinc_vs_epa_fem <- cinc_daily_map %>%
  inner_join(epa_hourly_daily_std, by = c("epa_site" = "Site", "date" = "Date")) %>%
  rename(pm25_cinc = pm25)

# C) CinC vs EPA FEM restricted to FRM days
frm_dates <- epa_24hr_daily_std %>% distinct(Date)
epa_fem_on_frm <- epa_hourly_daily_std %>% semi_join(frm_dates, by = "Date")

cinc_vs_epa_fem_on_frm <- cinc_daily_map %>%
  inner_join(epa_fem_on_frm, by = c("epa_site" = "Site", "date" = "Date")) %>%
  rename(pm25_cinc = pm25)
```

# CinC vs EPA PM2.5 comparisons

```{r}
# Reusable plotter: clean axes, per‑panel regression eq + R², and CSV export of the exact plotting table
plot_cinc_vs_epa <- function(df,
                             epa_col, cinc_col, site_col,
                             title_prefix, file_stub,
                             ncol = 3, q = 0.995) {

  stopifnot(all(c(epa_col, cinc_col, site_col) %in% names(df)))

  d <- df %>%
    transmute(epa_site = .data[[site_col]],
              pm_epa   = .data[[epa_col]],
              pm_cinc  = .data[[cinc_col]],
              date     = date,
              device_number = device_number,
              dist_km = dist_km) %>%
    filter(is.finite(pm_epa), is.finite(pm_cinc)) %>%
    mutate(epa_site = as.character(epa_site))

  if (!nrow(d)) return(invisible(NULL))

  # Robust common limits
  lim_hi <- quantile(c(d$pm_epa, d$pm_cinc), probs = q, na.rm = TRUE)
  lim_hi <- max(5, ceiling(lim_hi/5)*5)
  lims   <- c(0, lim_hi)

  # Per‑site regression
  site_stats <- d %>%
    group_by(epa_site) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      slope = if (n() > 2) coef(lm(pm_cinc ~ pm_epa))[2] else NA_real_,
      intercept = if (n() > 2) coef(lm(pm_cinc ~ pm_epa))[1] else NA_real_,
      r2 = if (n() > 2) summary(lm(pm_cinc ~ pm_epa))$r.squared else NA_real_,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      eq_label = ifelse(is.finite(r2),
                        sprintf("y = %.2fx %+ .2f\nR² = %.2f", slope, intercept, r2),
                        "n < 3"),
      x_lab = lims[1] + diff(lims)*0.03,
      y_lab = lims[2] - diff(lims)*0.06
    )

  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(d, aes(pm_epa, pm_cinc)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.0, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    coord_equal(xlim = lims, ylim = lims, expand = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ epa_site, ncol = ncol) +
    geom_text(data = site_stats, aes(x = x_lab, y = y_lab, label = eq_label),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
    labs(
      title = title_prefix,
      x = "EPA PM2.5 (µg/m³)",
      y = "CLEANinCLE PM2.5 (µg/m³)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(6)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.02),
      strip.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
      axis.title  = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.text   = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)

  # Save figure
  ggplot2::ggsave(paste0("figs/", file_stub, ".png"), p, width = 13, height = 10, dpi = 320)

  
  # Add the common limits used
  d_out <- d %>%
    mutate(axis_min = lims[1], axis_max = lims[2])

  readr::write_csv(d_out, paste0("figs/", file_stub, "_plotdata.csv"))

  # Also export per‑site regression summary
  readr::write_csv(site_stats %>%
                     select(epa_site, n, slope, intercept, r2) %>%
                     arrange(epa_site),
                   paste0("figs/", file_stub, "_site_stats.csv"))

  p
}
```

# CinC vs EPA PM2.5 comparison plots
```{r cinc-epa-plots, message=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=22}

# A) CinC vs EPA FRM
p_frm <- plot_cinc_vs_epa(
  df = cinc_vs_epa_frm,
  epa_col = "pm25_epa_frm",
  cinc_col = "pm25_cinc",
  site_col = "epa_site",
  title_prefix = "CLEANinCLE vs EPA FRM (24hr)-by EPA site",
  file_stub = "cinc_vs_epa_frm_by_site",
  ncol = 3
)

# B) CinC vs EPA FEM (daily avg)
p_fem <- plot_cinc_vs_epa(
  df = cinc_vs_epa_fem,
  epa_col = "pm25_epa_fem",
  cinc_col = "pm25_cinc",
  site_col = "epa_site",
  title_prefix = "CLEANinCLE vs EPA Continuous (daily avg)-by EPA site",
  file_stub = "cinc_vs_epa_fem_by_site",
  ncol = 3
)

# C) CinC vs EPA FEM on FRM days only
p_fem_on_frm <- plot_cinc_vs_epa(
  df = cinc_vs_epa_fem_on_frm,
  epa_col = "pm25_epa_fem",
  cinc_col = "pm25_cinc",
  site_col = "epa_site",
  title_prefix = "CLEANinCLE vs EPA Continuous (on FRM days)-by EPA site",
  file_stub = "cinc_vs_epa_fem_on_frm_by_site",
  ncol = 3
)

p_frm; p_fem; p_fem_on_frm
```

# Combined regression summary table (CinC vs EPA)
```{r}
# Combined table of regression results across the three comparisons
tbl_frm <- readr::read_csv("figs/cinc_vs_epa_frm_by_site_site_stats.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(comparison = "CinC vs EPA FRM")
tbl_fem <- readr::read_csv("figs/cinc_vs_epa_fem_by_site_site_stats.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(comparison = "CinC vs EPA FEM")
tbl_fem_on_frm <- readr::read_csv("figs/cinc_vs_epa_fem_on_frm_by_site_site_stats.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(comparison = "CinC vs EPA FEM on FRM days")

all_regressions <- bind_rows(tbl_frm, tbl_fem, tbl_fem_on_frm) %>%
  arrange(comparison, epa_site)

readr::write_csv(all_regressions, "figs/cinc_epa_all_regression_summaries.csv")
all_regressions

```

# CLEANinCLE vs EPA: Inter-site Agreement vs Distance
```{r}
library(geosphere)

# Function: compute R² and distance for each CinC–EPA pair
get_r2_dist <- function(df, epa_col, cinc_col, site_col, device_col = "device_number") {
  df %>%
    filter(is.finite(.data[[epa_col]]), is.finite(.data[[cinc_col]])) %>%
    group_by(pair = paste(.data[[site_col]], .data[[device_col]], sep = "-")) %>%
    summarise(
      epa_site = first(.data[[site_col]]),
      device   = first(.data[[device_col]]),
      r2 = if (n() > 2) summary(lm(.data[[cinc_col]] ~ .data[[epa_col]]))$r.squared else NA_real_,
      dist_km = mean(dist_km, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(r2), !is.na(dist_km))
}

# R² vs distance for all 3 comparisons
r2_frm        <- get_r2_dist(cinc_vs_epa_frm,        "pm25_epa_frm", "pm25_cinc", "epa_site")
r2_fem        <- get_r2_dist(cinc_vs_epa_fem,        "pm25_epa_fem", "pm25_cinc", "epa_site")
r2_fem_on_frm <- get_r2_dist(cinc_vs_epa_fem_on_frm, "pm25_epa_fem", "pm25_cinc", "epa_site")

# Add comparison labels
r2_frm$comparison        <- "CinC vs EPA FRM"
r2_fem$comparison        <- "CinC vs EPA FEM"
r2_fem_on_frm$comparison <- "CinC vs EPA FEM on FRM days"

r2_all <- bind_rows(r2_frm, r2_fem, r2_fem_on_frm)
```

# CinC–EPA Agreement by Distance (Bar Chart)
```{r}
# Assign bins
r2_all <- r2_all %>%
  mutate(dist_bin = case_when(
    dist_km <= 0.5       ~ "< 0.5 km (collocated)",
    dist_km <= 3         ~ "0.5–3 km (nearby)",
    dist_km > 3          ~ "> 3 km (farther)",
    TRUE                 ~ NA_character_
  ))

# Summary per bin + comparison
r2_summary <- r2_all %>%
  group_by(comparison, dist_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_r2 = mean(r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_pairs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot
p_bins <- ggplot(r2_summary, aes(x = dist_bin, y = avg_r2, fill = comparison)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "CinC–EPA Agreement by Distance",
    x = "Distance",
    y = expression("Average R"^2),
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

ggsave("figs/fig_r2_by_distance_bin.png", p_bins, width = 8, height = 5.5, dpi = 320)
p_bins
```

# CLEANinCLE Pairwise Comparism matrix
```{r cinc-16x16-matrix, fig.width=22, fig.height=22, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)


# Keep only numeric device columns (devices) and drop very sparse ones
cinc_mat <- cinc_wide %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  select(where(~ mean(!is.na(.)) > 0.7))

dev_ids <- names(cinc_mat)
n_dev   <- length(dev_ids)

# Axis range & ticks (adjust if you want)
axis_min <- 0
axis_max <- 40
tick_by  <- 10
tick_seq <- seq(axis_min, axis_max, by = tick_by)

# One panel builder (axis titles shown on **every** panel)
make_panel <- function(df_pair, x_lab_txt, y_lab_txt) {
  if (nrow(df_pair) > 2) {
    m  <- lm(y ~ x, data = df_pair)
    s  <- summary(m)
    r2 <- round(s$r.squared, 2)
    slope <- round(coef(m)[2], 2)
    intercept <- round(coef(m)[1], 2)
    eq  <- paste0("y = ", slope, "x + ", intercept, "\nR² = ", r2)
  } else {
    eq  <- "n < 3"
  }

  ggplot(df_pair, aes(x, y)) +
    geom_point(color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.55, size = 1.1) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    coord_fixed(xlim = c(axis_min, axis_max), ylim = c(axis_min, axis_max)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = tick_seq, limits = c(axis_min, axis_max)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = tick_seq, limits = c(axis_min, axis_max)) +
    annotate("text",
      x = axis_min + 0.8, y = axis_max - 0.8,
      label = eq, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 2.3
    ) +
    labs(
      x = paste0("Device ", x_lab_txt, " — PM2.5 (µg/m³)"),
      y = paste0("Device ", y_lab_txt, " — PM2.5 (µg/m³)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 7.5) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6.2, margin = margin(t = 4)),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6.2, margin = margin(r = 4)),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 5.8),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 5.8)
    )
}

# Build all panels (every panel shows its own axis titles with "Device <id>")
plots <- vector("list", n_dev * n_dev)

for (i in seq_len(n_dev)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n_dev)) {
    x_id <- dev_ids[j]
    y_id <- dev_ids[i]
    idx  <- (i - 1) * n_dev + j

    df_pair <- cinc_mat %>%
      select(x = all_of(x_id), y = all_of(y_id)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y))

    plots[[idx]] <- make_panel(df_pair, x_lab_txt = x_id, y_lab_txt = y_id)
  }
}

matrix_plot <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots, ncol = n_dev, nrow = n_dev)

# Save large canvas so labels remain readable
dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
w <- max(20, n_dev * 2.2)   # width in inches
h <- max(22, n_dev * 2.5)   # height in inches
ggsave("figs/fig_cinc_pairwise_device_full_labels.png", matrix_plot, width = w, height = h, dpi = 300)

matrix_plot
```

# CinC-EPA R² summary tables 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# sanity checks (these should already exist from your pipeline)
("cinc_vs_epa_cont_on_filter_days")
("epa_hourly_daily_std")
(exists("epa_24hr_daily_std"))
(exists("cinc_daily_map"))

# helper to compute R² safely
r2_safe <- function(x, y) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
  if (sum(ok) > 2) summary(lm(y[ok] ~ x[ok]))$r.squared else NA_real_
}

# 1) CinC vs EPA Continuous (FEM) restricted to FRM days  -------------------
# (You already built: cinc_vs_epa_cont_on_filter_days with pm25_cinc, pm25_epa_cont)

r2_cont_on_frm <- cinc_vs_epa_cont_on_filter_days %>%
  group_by(epa_site) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs = sum(complete.cases(pm25_cinc, pm25_epa_cont)),
    r2      = r2_safe(pm25_epa_cont, pm25_cinc),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(comparison = "CinC vs EPA Continuous (on FRM days)")

# 2) CinC vs EPA Filter (FRM) restricted to FEM days  -----------------------
# Filter dates down to those where FEM (continuous) exists
fem_dates <- epa_hourly_daily_std %>% distinct(Date)

epa_filter_on_fem <- epa_24hr_daily_std %>%  # has Date, Site, pm25_epa
  semi_join(fem_dates, by = "Date")

# Join CinC daily with EPA FRM on FEM days
cinc_vs_epa_filter_on_fem_days <- cinc_daily_map %>%        # has device -> epa_site mapping + CinC daily
  inner_join(epa_filter_on_fem, by = c("epa_site" = "Site", "date" = "Date")) %>%
  rename(pm25_cinc = pm25, pm25_epa_filter = pm25_epa)

r2_filter_on_fem <- cinc_vs_epa_filter_on_fem_days %>%
  group_by(epa_site) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs = sum(complete.cases(pm25_cinc, pm25_epa_filter)),
    r2      = r2_safe(pm25_epa_filter, pm25_cinc),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(comparison = "CinC vs EPA Filter (on FEM days)")

# 3) Combine + save (long and wide formats) ---------------------------------

r2_long <- bind_rows(r2_cont_on_frm, r2_filter_on_fem) %>%
  arrange(epa_site, comparison) %>%
  mutate(r2 = round(r2, 3))

r2_wide <- r2_long %>%
  select(epa_site, comparison, r2) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = comparison,
    values_from = r2
  )

# write out
dir.create("figs", showWarnings = FALSE)
write_csv(r2_long, "figs/cinc_epa_r2_long_FRMvsFEM_restricted.csv")
write_csv(r2_wide, "figs/cinc_epa_r2_wide_FRMvsFEM_restricted.csv")

# preview
r2_long
r2_wide
```
